source_text: Upon transmission or receipt of a fatal alert message, both parties MUST immediately close the connection.
rule_statement: Upon sending or receiving a fatal alert, both parties MUST immediately close the connection.
applies_when: Fatal alert is sent or received
checkability_rationale: Can verify connection is closed after fatal alert.

source_text: Whenever an implementation encounters a fatal error condition, it SHOULD send an appropriate fatal alert and MUST close the connection without sending or receiving any additional data.
rule_statement: On fatal error, implementation MUST close connection without sending or receiving additional data.
expected_value: no additional data after fatal error
checkability_rationale: Can verify no data is sent/received after error alert.

source_text: The phrases "terminate the connection with an X alert" and "abort the handshake with an X alert" mean that the implementation MUST send alert X if it sends any alert.
rule_statement: When spec says terminate/abort with alert X, implementation MUST send alert X if it sends any alert.
applies_when: Implementation terminates connection where specific alert is mandated
checkability_rationale: Can verify correct alert type is sent for each error condition.

source_text: Peers which receive a message which cannot be parsed according to the syntax (e.g., have a length extending beyond the message boundary or contain an out-of-range length) MUST terminate the connection with a "decode_error" alert.
rule_statement: Messages that cannot be parsed syntactically MUST trigger decode_error alert.
checkability_rationale: Can send malformed messages and verify decode_error response.

source_text: Peers which receive a message which is syntactically correct but semantically invalid (e.g., a DHE share of p - 1, or an invalid enum) MUST terminate the connection with an "illegal_parameter" alert.
rule_statement: Messages that are syntactically correct but semantically invalid MUST trigger illegal_parameter alert.
checkability_rationale: Can send semantically invalid messages and verify illegal_parameter response.
